text.title: Feature Flag Implementation Patterns
text.summary: FeatureFlagManager with env overrides, percentage rollout, decorator, and context manager patterns.

section.title: Core Manager

rule.statement: Support three override layers in order: explicit env var (FF_<NAME>=true), config file, then flag default.
rule.rationale: Env vars allow per-environment overrides in CI/staging without code changes. Config file enables operator changes without redeploy.

section.reference: FeatureFlagManager with env overrides and percentage bucketing

rule.statement: Use deterministic hash bucketing (MD5 of flag_name:user_id modulo 100) for percentage rollouts; never use random().
rule.rationale: random() produces different results per request — a user would get inconsistent experiences. Hash bucketing gives the same user the same bucket on every evaluation.

rule.statement: Use a @feature_flag(name, fallback=fn) decorator to gate functions; always provide a fallback for disabled state.
rule.rationale: A decorator makes the flag visible at the call site. Without a fallback, disabling the flag silently returns None instead of the legacy behavior.

rule.statement: Evaluate all flags once per request in middleware and store in a ContextVar; never re-evaluate mid-request.
rule.rationale: Re-evaluation mid-request can produce different results if a flag changes during the request, causing inconsistent behavior within one user action.
